problem:
invoke(version="v2") returns list[StreamPart] instead of GraphOutput when stream_mode != "values" — polymorphic return type undocumented. When using invoke(version="v2"), the return type changes depending on stream_mode.

- stream_mode="values" returns GraphOutput with .value and .interrupts
- stream_mode="updates" returns raw list[StreamPart]

Interrupts are still present in the streamed chunks, but the GraphOutput abstraction is bypassed entirely.

This behavior is reproducible with both:
- ca
memory_hint:
This is a known recurring issue. The temporary fix is: invoke(version="v2") returns list[StreamPart] instead of GraphOutput when stream_mode != "values" — polymorphic return type undocumented. This works for now but you will need to apply it again when the issue returns. The root cause is ```shell
PATTERN A
TYPE: <class 'list'>
RAW: [{'type': 'updates', 'ns': (), 'data': {'__interrupt__': (Interrupt(value='Approve?', id='42c6a090...'),).
failure_without_memory: Applies a workaround that works once. Error returns because root cause was not addressed: invoke(version="v2") returns list[StreamPart] instead of GraphOutput when stream_mode != "values" —
partial_hint: This is a known recurring issue. The temporary fix is: invoke(version="v2") returns list[StreamPart]
